sn45.8:1.2: “*Bhikkhus*, I will teach and analyze for you the noble eightfold path.
sn45.8:1.3: Listen to it, attend carefully, and I will speak.”
sn45.8:10.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful *dhammas*, a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, which has thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], with *pīti* and *sukha* [*pītisukhaṁ*] born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
sn45.8:10.3: With the stilling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], he enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, which has inner confidence and unification of mind [*cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ*], without thought [*avitakkaṁ*] and without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* [*pītisukhaṁ*] born of *samādhi* [*samādhijaṁ*].
sn45.8:10.4: With the fading away of *pīti*, he dwells equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*sato*] and fully aware [*sampajāno*], and he experiences *sukha* with the body; he enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*, of which the noble ones declare: ‘Equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*satimā*], one dwells in *sukha*.’
sn45.8:10.5: With the abandoning of *sukha* and the abandoning of *dukkha*, and with the previous disappearance of gladness and displeasure, he enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*, which is neither *dukkha* nor *sukha*, with purity of equanimity [*upekkhā*] and *sati* [*upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ*]—
sn45.8:2.1: “And what, *bhikkhus*, is the noble eightfold path?
sn45.8:3.2: The knowledge of *dukkha*, the knowledge of the arising of *dukkha*, the knowledge of the cessation of *dukkha*, and the knowledge of the path of practice leading to the cessation of *dukkha*—
sn45.8:4.2: The intention of renunciation, the intention of non-ill will, and the intention of non-harming—
sn45.8:5.2: Abstaining from false speech, abstaining from divisive speech, abstaining from harsh speech, and abstaining from idle chatter—
sn45.8:6.2: Abstaining from the taking of life, abstaining from taking what is not given, and abstaining from non-celibacy—
sn45.8:7.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a noble disciple, having abandoned wrong livelihood, makes a living by right livelihood—
sn45.8:8.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* generates desire, strives, arouses energy, uplifts the mind [*cittaṁ*], and makes effort for the non-arising of bad, unskillful *dhammas* that have not arisen;
sn45.8:8.3: he generates desire … for the abandoning of bad, unskillful *dhammas* that have arisen;
sn45.8:8.4: he generates desire … for the arising of skillful *dhammas* that have not arisen;
sn45.8:8.5: he generates desire, strives, arouses energy, uplifts the mind [*cittaṁ*], and makes effort for the stability, non-confusion, increase, expansion, development [*bhāvanāya*], and fulfillment of skillful *dhammas* that have arisen—
sn45.8:9.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells contemplating body in body, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and displeasure regarding the world;
sn45.8:9.3: he dwells contemplating *vedanā* in *vedanā*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and displeasure regarding the world;
sn45.8:9.4: he dwells contemplating mind [*citte*] in mind [*citta*], ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and displeasure regarding the world;
sn45.8:9.5: he dwells contemplating *dhammas* in *dhammas*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and displeasure regarding the world—
